more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Chinese Embassy Warns Investors After Zimbabwe Bans Lithium And Raw Mineral Exports
more.description: Chinese Embassy Warns Nationals As US$1.4 Billion In Lithium Investment Hangs In The Balance After Zimbabwe Ban The Chinese Embassy in Zimbabwe has warned its nationals and companies to exercise caution after Zimbabwe banned the export of lithium concentrates and raw minerals, a move that took immediate effect and affected minerals already in transit. The [...] The post Chinese Embassy Warns Investors After Zimbabwe Bans Lithium And Raw Mineral Exports appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: Warriors coach Marinica targets Four Nations Tournament to build future squad
more.description: Warriors coach Marian Marinica says he will use the upcoming Four Nations Tournament as a platform to build a competitive squad for future assignments. The Four Nations tournament is scheduled to run from March 28 to April 2 and will feature Zimbabwe, hosts Botswana, Malawi and Zambia. Marinica has, however, been dealt a major blow [...] The post Warriors coach Marinica targets Four Nations Tournament to build future squad appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Police hunt for woman forcing child to touch a blazing gas stove
more.description:
The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has asked for help in finding a woman who has been seen abusing a child.

more.title: Harare bookkeeper in court for externalising US$4 million
more.description: A 76-year-old Harare woman has appeared in court facing charges of externalisation and money laundering involving more than US$4 million. Maud Patricia Liddell appeared before magistrate Ruth Moyo, who granted her US$500 bail and ordered her to report to the police fortnightly as part of her bail conditions. Liddell, who is self-employed as a bookkeeper [...] The post Harare bookkeeper in court for externalising US$4 million appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Elderly Harare Woman Faces US$4m Money-Laundering Charges
more.description: ​By Judith Nyuke A 76-year-old Harare woman appeared at the Harare Magistrates’ Court after she reportedly transferred US$4 085 000 outside Zimbabwe without authorisation from the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe.​Maud Patricia Liddell appeared before Harare Magistrate Ruth Moyo charged with externalisation and contravening the Proceeds [...] The post Elderly Harare Woman Faces US$4m Money-Laundering Charges appeared first on 263Chat .

more.title: In Zimbabwe, an almost-deadly collision between fake news and a real virus
more.description: By Derick Matsengarwodzi, VaccinesWork One day in 2023, mother-of-one Memory Mukusha was attending to her toddler when a curious message popped up on her mobile. She stopped what she was doing to read the incoming message, flagged eye-catchingly as “breaking news”. At first glance, she took the tone of the text to be flatly educational. [...] The post In Zimbabwe, an almost-deadly collision between fake news and a real virus appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: The Mirage of Muzarabani: The Distance Between Discovery and Delivery
more.description: The Muzarabani gas discovery is being presented to Zimbabweans as a moment of arrival, a geological vindication that the country sits atop untapped energy wealth capable of transforming its economy.

more.title: Harare CBD Bus Terminals Overrun By Vendors, Street Kids, Homeless Adults
more.description: Harare’s Central Business District (CBD) bus terminals are increasingly descending into chaos, with vendors, street children, and homeless adults turning them into makeshift settlements, raising serious safety and sanitation concerns for commuters.

more.title: 5 000t rice boosts food security
more.description: Source: 5 000t rice boosts food security – herald Precious Manomano and Muchaneta Chimuka Zimbabwe has received 5 000 tonnes of rice from China as part of efforts to address food insecurity among vulnerable populations. more.title: Mutapa assets valued at ZiG16bn
more.description: Source: Mutapa assets valued at ZiG16bn – herald Farirai Machivenyika Senior Reporter The Mutapa Investment Fund’s gross assets were valued at ZiG16 billion as at December 31 last year, following a valuation exercise carried out by several independent auditors. This was revealed by MIF chief executive Dr John Mangudya yesterday when he appeared before Parliament’s [...] The post Mutapa assets valued at ZiG16bn appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Who will drive the bus: Mamvura, General Chiwenga, or someone else? Will President Mnangagwa retire peacefully? (Part 2)
more.description: In this second and final part of the article, I continue to examine the potential outcomes of ZANU-PF’s succession politics, focusing on whether Kudakwashe Tagwirei (whom I metaphorically refer to as “Mamvura”) will succeed in his presumed bid for the presidency, whether General Constantino Chiwenga will recover his political standing and take over, whether someone else will ascend to the throne, and whether President Mnangagwa will ultimately retire in peace. The post Who will drive the bus: Mamvura, General Chiwenga, or someone else? Will President Mnangagwa retire peacefully? (Part 2) appeared first on Nehanda Radio .
